watchers.name: cli-commands
watchers.detect: New or renamed command names from `program.command()` / Commander definitions. New or removed flags from `.option()`. Argument signature changes. Help-text changes.

watchers.name: env-vars
watchers.detect: Lines matching `^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*=` in .env.example; `process.env.X` reads; zod env schema fields. Append-only edits are auto-merge; renames/removals escalate to PR tier.

watchers.name: rest-routes
watchers.detect: Route registrations (`app.get/post/put/delete`, fastify `route({...})`). New route file = new doc page; signature/payload changes = update existing page.

watchers.name: adapters
watchers.detect: New top-level directory under either path = new adapter; create a new doc page mirroring the neighbour pattern. Manifest (`package.json`, `adapter.json`) changes = update existing page. When a new top-level dir appears, before treating it as a new adapter, check whether a comparably-named dir was removed in the same window (rename heuristic). Use git's rename detection signal in the compare response (`status: 'renamed'` on individual files, or correlated add/remove of similar paths — especially common within the `*-local` adapter family). If rename: update the existing doc page in place, do not create a new page.

watchers.name: plugin-sdk
watchers.detect: Changes to exported types/functions from `src/index.ts` (public API surface). Internal-only changes ignored.

watchers.name: schemas
watchers.detect: Zod / drizzle schema field additions, removals, or type changes on entities surfaced in docs (companies, agents, issues, routines, approvals, secrets).

watchers.name: web-ui
watchers.detect: UI component changes that may invalidate screenshots or end-user-facing guide flows. Cross-reference with screenshot registry depends_on entries before writing doc edits.

watchers.name: server-services
watchers.detect: Business logic changes that surface behavioural shifts users will notice (e.g. how agents interact, how approvals flow). Only produce doc edits when the change has user-visible effects, not pure refactors.

watchers.name: server-adapters
watchers.detect: Server-side adapter integrations (distinct from packages/adapters/*, which are the standalone adapter packages). Often relates to how the server hosts/proxies adapter capabilities.

watchers.name: db-migrations
watchers.detect: Migrations themselves rarely need user-facing docs, but a sudden burst of migrations often correlates with a schema change watched elsewhere. Use this watcher's hits as context only — never produce edits directly.

watchers.name: release-notes
watchers.detect: Fetched via `gh release view <tag>` to give other watchers framing/intent. Never produces edits directly — release notes are reference data, not source.

auto_merge_safety.$comment: forbid_kinds blocks auto-merge for destructive changes regardless of size. A 1-line rename is still a breaking change and demands human review. max_lines_changed=50 accommodates real-world env-vars batch additions (e.g. a new feature shipping with 10–20 related env vars and their inline comments in .env.example).

$notes.no_skip_patterns: We deliberately do NOT filter commits by message regex (e.g. ^revert, ^[codex]). The diff window is cumulative (last_release_tag → HEAD) so reverts are already net-zero in the manifest. Filtering by message would HIDE the undo signal — if we processed an original commit and then skipped its revert, docs would stay wrong forever. The proper filter is surface impact via watcher path globs.
$notes.quarantine_still_needed: Quarantine handles the race where a feature lands but its revert hasn't yet. Without it, we'd churn doc edits that get undone the next day.
